MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
James F. Hannawalt of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3913) for defendant and moving party; Heather G. Hensley of the Law Office of Daniel Berko (415.771.6174) for plaintiff and responding party.

Discussed and the Court rules as follows: summary judgment is granted on the grounds that there is no evidence of constructive knowledge of a dangerous condition. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Continued To Dec-01-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Berko, Esq. (415-771-6174) for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Bers, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-4224) of Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the 2 issues Plaintiff raised in his email; extend trial and due process problem. Plaintiff argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tenatative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is continued to December 1, 2017. Plaintiff Larry Littlejohn has until November 29, 2017 to file a declaration from Ellisse Penn (aka Esther Pennington) that shows constructive notice of the allegedly dangerous condition by the City. If Mr. Littlejohn does not do so, the City's motion will be granted. There will no further continuances of this motion absent extraordinary unanticipated circumstances. To the extent that Mr. Littlejohn requests that the Department 302 judge continue the trial and/or issue an order compelling the Mary Elizabeth Inn to allow access to Mr. Littlejohn's agents, the requests are denied. No good cause has been shown for a trial continuance and Mary Elizabeth Inn has not been given notice or an opportunity to oppose the request for access. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
